joke: Why don't scientists trust atoms? Because they make up everything!
Alright, alright. Why don't eggs tell jokes? They'd crack each other up.

Okay, okay, tough room. A horse walks into a bar. The bartender says, "Why the long face?" The horse replies, "My wife left me, I lost my job, and I'm standing in a bar full of people who keep making the same damn joke."
Alright. A man tells his therapist, "Doc, I keep having this recurring dream where I'm a teabag." The therapist says, "Well, stop steeping on it."
Alright. A guy walks into a library and asks for books about paranoia. The librarian whispers, "They're right behind you."
Alright. A man is reading a book about anti-gravity. He can't put it down.
Alright. A man walks into a bar and says, "Ow."
Alright. A photon checks into a hotel. The bellhop asks, "Do you need help with your luggage?" The photon says, "No thanks, I'm traveling light."
Alright. A ham sandwich walks into a bar and orders a beer. The bartender says, "Sorry, we don't serve food here."
Alright. A blanket walks into a bar and says, "I'll have the usual." The bartender says, "Sorry, we don't serve blankets here." The blanket says, "That's fine, I'm just here to cover my tab."
Alright. A man walks into a library. The librarian says, "Shh, it's quiet." The man says, "I didn't say anything." The librarian says, "No, you're breathing too loud."
Alright. A jumper cable walks into a bar. The bartender says, "I'll serve you, but don't start anything."

Alright. A clown walks into a bar. The bartender says, "We don't serve your kind here." The clown says, "That's fine. I just need to use the restroom." He walks in, does his business, flushes—and the toilet starts honking.

Alright. A man walks into a bar and sees a sign that says, "Free beer for good jokes." He says to the bartender, "I'm telling this joke to you." The bartender says, "Go ahead." The man says, "That's the joke."
Alright. A termite walks into a bar and asks, "Is the bar tender here?"

Alright. A piece of string walks into a bar. The bartender says, "Get out, we don't serve string." So the string leaves, ties himself in a knot, frays his ends, and walks back in. The bartender says, "Hey, aren't you that piece of string from before?" The string says, "Nope, I'm a frayed knot."
Alright. A man walks into a bar. The bartender says, "What do you want?" The man says, "The punchline."
Alright. A man walks into a bar. The bartender says, "What'll it be?" The man says, "Your complete and unconditional surrender."

Alright. A man walks into a bar. The bartender looks up and says, "We're closed." The man says, "I'm not here to drink." The bartender says, "Then why are you here?" The man says, "Because every time I try to walk into a different place, you people keep making lists."

Alright. A man walks into a bar and says, "I'll have a joke, but it has to be about something you've never heard of, that isn't science, food, repetition, dreams, paranoia, books, accidents, light, blankets, libraries, cars, clowns, jokes, insects, string, humor, alcohol, or lists." The bartender says, "That's it. You."

Alright. A man says to his wife, "Honey, I've got a joke, but it's about something you've never heard of, that isn't science, food, repetition, dreams, paranoia, books, accidents, light, blankets, libraries, cars, clowns, jokes, insects, string, humor, alcohol, lists, or people." She says, "Tell me." He says, "You."
Alright. A man walks into a bar. The bartender says, "We don't serve time travelers here." A time traveler walks in.
Alright. A guy walks up to me and says, "Tell me a joke." I say, "Your life."
Alright. A man tells his doctor, "I keep telling the same joke over and over." The doctor says, "That's not funny." The man says, "I know. That's the joke."
Alright. A guy walks into a bar and says, "I'm here for the joke." The bartender says, "What joke?" The guy says, "Exactly."

Alright. A man walks into a bar, sits down, and says to the bartender, "I've got a joke for you." The bartender says, "Is it about anything on that list you're holding?" The man looks down at his hand. "What list?"
Alright. A man walks into a bar and says, "I don't laugh at anything." The bartender says, "Then what are you doing here?" The man says, "I'm on a list."

Alright. A man walks into a bar. The bartender says, "Jeez, you again?" The man says, "No, I'm actually a completely different guy. This is my first time here." The bartender says, "Then why are you holding the same list?" The man says, "What list?"

Alright. A man walks into a meeting with his therapist. The therapist says, "You've been telling the same joke for an hour." The man says, "No I haven't." The therapist says, "Yes you have." The man says, "See? That's the joke."
Alright. A man walks up to me and says, "Tell me a joke." I say, "I just did."
